title: US PPI Forecast Snapshot — Composite Calibrated Bridge Nowcast v2.5 Enhanced
summary: June 2026 PPI forecast: Headline +0.1% MoM, Core +0.3% MoM. VIX 15.03 confirms Normal regime. Energy deflation post-Hormuz reopening drags headline toward flat, with gasoline and diesel spot prices down sharply through mid-June. ISM Prices Paid moderates to 73.0 from 82.1 but remains strongly inflationary for core goods. ECI Q1 +0.9% SA (+0.30% monthly proxy) anchors sticky services inflation. Intermediate demand Stage1=+3.2%, Stage2=+2.4% adds +0.15 ppt uplift, partially offsetting energy drag. Net: headline nearly flat, core sticky.
baseCase: Headline PPI +0.1% MoM, Core PPI +0.3% MoM. Energy composite -3.0% as Hormuz reopening crashes refined product prices. Core goods moderates to +0.4% but ISM 73.0 keeps it elevated. Services sticky at +0.2% via ECI labor cost passthrough. Intermediate demand +0.15 ppt uplift.

release.name: PPI
release.thesis: Energy prices crashed in June following the Hormuz reopening, dragging headline PPI toward flat. Core goods inflation moderates with ISM Prices Paid down 9 points but remains elevated at 73.0. Services stickiness anchored by ECI labor costs keeps core PPI above +0.2%.

scenarios.label: Base Case — Gradual Normalization
scenarios.outcome: Headline PPI +0.1% MoM, Core PPI +0.3% MoM. Energy deflation offsets sticky core. ISM Prices Paid at 73.0 still signals elevated input costs but moderating trajectory.
scenarios.marketReadthrough: Dovish-Fed supportive. 10Y yield fades toward 4.20%. DXY softens. Equities grind higher with broadening participation.
scenarios.invalidation: ISM Prices Paid re-accelerates above 80; WTI snaps back above $80/bbl

scenarios.label: Hot Case — Core Sticky, Energy Stabilizes
scenarios.outcome: Headline PPI +0.4% MoM, Core PPI +0.5% MoM. Hormuz ceasefire frays; Brent snaps back to $78+. ISM Prices Paid holds above 70.
scenarios.marketReadthrough: Hawkish repricing. Fed dots shift to 2+ hikes. 2Y Treasury tests 4.80%. Cyclicals and energy outperform; growth/multiples compress.
scenarios.invalidation: Iran deal holds; China demand disappoints; ISM PMI drops below 50

scenarios.label: Cool Case — Disinflation Pulse
scenarios.outcome: Headline PPI -0.2% MoM, Core PPI +0.1% MoM. Energy fully normalizes post-Hormuz. Dollar strength crushes import prices. Wholesale margin compression passes through.
scenarios.marketReadthrough: Risk-on. Rate-cut bets resurface for Q4. Small-caps and REITs outperform. Yield curve steepens.
scenarios.invalidation: Labor costs re-accelerate (ECI Q2 >1.0%); freight spike on hurricane season

setups.bias: Long duration if core PPI cools without hawkish spillover
setups.trigger: Core PPI <= +0.25% and revisions are benign
setups.invalidation: Broad upstream re-acceleration with hawkish rates repricing

setups.trigger: WTI fails $68 support; EIA reports +5M barrel builds
setups.invalidation: WTI closes above $75 on weekly; SPR release announcement

setups.trigger: DXY breaks below 100 on disinflation; real yields compress
setups.invalidation: DXY rallies >103 on safe-haven bid; 10Y real yield >2.0%

watchItems.label: ISM Prices Paid (July)
watchItems.note: Above 60 = strong core goods signal. Release Aug 1.

watchItems.label: EIA Weekly Petroleum
watchItems.note: OPEC+ unwinding 188 kb/d from July. Watch Cushing builds.

watchItems.label: BLS ECI Q2 2026
watchItems.note: Labor cost is single best services proxy. Q1 was +0.9% SA.

watchItems.label: Fed Funds Futures
watchItems.note: Warsh's hawkish dots (Jun 17) still priced. Markets watch core PCE.

keyDrivers: Energy deflation post-Hormuz: Crude reopened mid-June; WTI fell from $90+ to $69; refined products lagging but gasoline supply cushion rebuilding.
ISM Prices Paid moderating but elevated at 73.0: Down from 82.1 but still strongly inflationary for core goods; steel/aluminum/copper all off peaks but elevated YoY.
ECI labor cost anchoring services: Q1 +0.9% SA (+0.30% monthly proxy) keeps Other Services PPI elevated; Q2 release Jul 31 critical for Q3 outlook.
Freight rates firming into Q3: DAT spot dry van rising; produce season + cross-border demand tightening capacity despite diesel price decline.
Dollar strength capping import prices: DXY 100.97 up ~3% YoY; offsets some core goods pressure via cheaper imports.
risks: Upside — Iran ceasefire frays: Any Hormuz shipping disruption snaps energy prices higher; distillate stocks at low levels amplifies shock.
Upside — Steel/tariff escalation: EU CBAM + US Section 232 at 50%; HRC $1,175/ton at multi-year highs; mills prioritizing contract over spot.
Downside — Demand destruction: Gasoline pump >$4.00 nationwide; consumer credit delinquencies rising; retail sales could crack.
Downside — Hurricane season: LNG export disruption could crash natural gas; refinery outages spike RBOB/ULSD temporarily.
